root_text.an5.214:1.3: Musā bhaṇati, pisuṇaṁ bhaṇati, pharusaṁ bhaṇati, samphappalāpaṁ bhaṇati, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an5.214:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ādīnavā bahubhāṇismiṁ puggale.
root_text.an5.214:2.3: Na musā bhaṇati, na pisuṇaṁ bhaṇati, na pharusaṁ bhaṇati, na samphappalāpaṁ bhaṇati, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an5.214:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ānisaṁsā mantabhāṇismiṁ puggale”ti.

translation_text.an5.214:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five drawbacks for an individual who talks a lot.
translation_text.an5.214:1.3: They use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, and nonsensical. When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an5.214:1.4: These are the five drawbacks for an individual who talks a lot.
translation_text.an5.214:2.1: There are these five benefits for an individual who talks thoughtfully.
translation_text.an5.214:2.3: They don’t use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, and nonsensical. When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an5.214:2.4: These are the five benefits for an individual who talks thoughtfully.”
